mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects rising labor income and aggregate demand, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive 20-day return rate when PAYEMS is rising does not exceed the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% represents restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative 60-day return rate when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% is not significantly higher than the unconditional negative rate (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and decline in unemployment jointly confirm demand-driven expansion with labor market validation, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days than either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: In periods where RSAFS rises and UNRATE falls, the positive 20-day return rate is not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and operating leverage that boost profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, rising INDPRO does not produce a positive 60-day return rate significantly above the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in unemployment of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that undermines consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative 5-day return rate when the trigger fires is not significantly higher than the unconditional negative rate (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate does not decline alongside falling CPI inflation, the resulting increase in ex-ante real policy rates tightens financial conditions and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of non-declining FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL does not produce a negative 5-day return rate significantly above the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term fails to improve prediction over main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,000 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative 60-day return rate when HOUST is below 1,000 is not significantly higher than the unconditional negative rate (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 2% signals acute negative operating leverage and earnings compression across the manufacturing sector, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative 20-day return rate when INDPRO contracts by more than 2% over 3 months is not significantly higher than the unconditional negative rate (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains above -0.03.
